tags.name: Chain Endpoints
tags.description: Chain endpoints to discover supported blockchains and their capabilities

tags.name: Account Endpoints
tags.description: Account endpoints to retrieve OpenSea account profiles and resolve identifiers

tags.name: Collection Endpoints
tags.description: Collection endpoints to retrieve collection details, traits, and trending data

tags.name: NFT Endpoints
tags.description: NFT endpoints to retrieve individual NFTs, metadata, ownership, and rarity

tags.name: Contract Endpoints
tags.description: Contract endpoints to retrieve contract metadata and payment token details

tags.name: Token Endpoints
tags.description: Token endpoints for getting token details, balances, and trending data

tags.name: Search Endpoints
tags.description: Search endpoints for discovering collections, tokens, NFTs, and accounts

tags.name: Listing Endpoints
tags.description: Listing endpoints for creating, fulfilling, and querying listings

tags.name: Offer Endpoints
tags.description: Offer endpoints for building, creating, fulfilling, and querying offers

tags.name: Order Endpoints
tags.description: Order endpoints for looking up and canceling individual orders

tags.name: Swap Endpoints
tags.description: Swap endpoints for getting token swap quotes with executable transaction data

tags.name: Drops Endpoints
tags.description: Endpoints for discovering drops, checking mint eligibility, and building mint transactions

tags.name: Analytics Endpoints
tags.description: Analytics endpoints to retrieve events, stats, and trading data

tags.name: Tool Endpoints [Beta]
tags.description: [Beta] Tool endpoints for discovering and inspecting registered onchain tools. This API is under active development and may change without notice.

tags.name: Transaction Endpoints
tags.description: Transaction endpoints for checking transaction receipts and status

post.description: Get the receipt/status for a submitted transaction. Works for all transaction types including listing fulfillments, cross-chain buys, sweeps, offer fulfillments, and token swaps. Poll this endpoint after submitting transactions to check completion status.

[Beta] Report verified tool usage for metrics and analytics purposes only. Supports verification_type: eip3009_authorization (free tools, identity proof) or x402_settlement (paid tools, onchain USDC payment verification). This endpoint does NOT trigger any onchain transactions. This endpoint is under active development and may change without notice.
Retrieve multiple tokens in a single request by providing a list of contract identifiers (chain and address). Not-found tokens are silently omitted from the response.
Get executable transactions for token-to-token swaps. Supports same-chain and cross-chain swaps with multiple from/to assets.
Create an offer to purchase a single NFT (ERC721 or ERC1155).
List a single NFT (ERC721 or ERC1155) for sale on the OpenSea marketplace.

Offchain cancel a single order, offer or listing, by its order hash when protected by the SignedZone. Protocol and Chain are required to prevent hash collisions. Please note cancellation is only assured if a fulfillment signature was not vended prior to cancellation.

Create a criteria offer to purchase any NFT in a collection or which matches the specified trait. For trait offers where the Build Offer endpoint returns identifierOrCriteria '0', use that value directly — trait matching is validated server-side at fulfillment time rather than via onchain merkle proof.

Retrieve all the information, including signatures, needed to fulfill an offer directly onchain. For trait offers with identifierOrCriteria '0', the server validates that the specified token matches the offer's trait criteria before generating fulfillment data.

400.description:
The request is invalid
The order_hash does not exist
The chain is not an EVM Chain
The protocol_address is not a supported Seaport contract
For other error reasons, see the response data.

post.description: Build a portion of a criteria offer including the consideration item, zone, and zone hash needed to post an offer. For trait offers on supported collections, the identifierOrCriteria in the returned consideration will be '0' (no merkle root computation needed). For other collections, a computed merkle root is returned. When identifierOrCriteria is '0', the encodedTokenIds field is informational only and not required for constructing the onchain order.
Retrieve multiple NFTs in a single request by providing a list of identifiers (chain, contract address, and token ID). Not-found NFTs are silently omitted from the response.

post.summary: Sweep buy items from a collection
post.description: Buy up to N items from a collection using any payment token, including cross-chain. If a requested item becomes unavailable, the system can automatically substitute it with the next cheapest listing from the same collection (enabled by default). Returns an ordered list of transactions to execute.

400.description: The request is invalid. Possible reasons: collection not found, invalid chain, max_items exceeds limit, or no listings available.

post.description: Retrieve all the information, including signatures, needed to fulfill a listing directly onchain.

400.description:
The request is invalid
The order_hash does not exist
The chain is not an EVM Chain
The protocol_address is not a supported Seaport contract
For other error reasons, see the response data.

post.summary: Fulfill a listing using a different token
post.description: Get fulfillment data to buy one or more listings using a token on a different chain or a different token on the same chain. Supports cross-chain purchases and same-chain token swaps via the Relay protocol. Returns an ordered list of transactions to execute.

400.description: The request is invalid. Possible reasons: listing not found, listing not valid, listing is not a listing order, or no fulfillment actions could be generated.

post.description: Returns the blockchain actions (approvals + Seaport order signing payload) needed to list one or more NFTs. This eliminates the need to construct Seaport orders manually.

400.description: The request is invalid. Possible reasons: items on different chains, invalid addresses, items not found, invalid currency, or prices/quantities not positive.

post.summary: Build mint transaction data for a drop
post.description: Returns ready-to-sign transaction data for minting tokens from a drop. The caller is responsible for signing and submitting the transaction. No wallet authentication is required — only an API key. The minter address in the request body determines who will receive the tokens. Stage selection is handled automatically by the backend — if multiple stages are active, the first eligible stage is used.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: The collection slug identifying the drop

200.description: Ready-to-sign transaction data including target contract, calldata, and value

400.description: Invalid request: bad address format, invalid quantity, or missing required fields

404.description: Drop or collection not found for the given slug

409.description: Drop is not currently active for minting (not started, ended, or paused)

422.description: Minting precondition failed: wallet not in allowlist, mint limit exceeded, or supply exhausted

post.description: Returns ready-to-sign transaction data for deploying a new NFT drop contract. The caller is responsible for signing and submitting the transaction.

200.description: Ready-to-sign transaction data for contract deployment

400.description: Invalid request: unsupported drop_type/token_type combination, bad address format, or missing required fields

post.description: Retrieve multiple collections in a single request by providing a list of slugs. Results are returned in the same order as the input slugs. Not-found collections are silently omitted.

Fetch and validate NFT metadata directly from the blockchain without using cached data. Returns both original and processed (SeaDN) URLs to show how the metadata would be ingested. This endpoint does not persist any data.

parameters.name: chain
parameters.description: The blockchain on which the NFT exists

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: The contract address

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: The NFT token id

parameters.name: ignoreCachedItemUrls
parameters.description: Whether to bypass cached SeaDN URLs

post.description: Queue a metadata refresh for a specific NFT to update its information from the blockchain.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Contract address

parameters.name: chain
parameters.description: Blockchain chain identifier

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Token identifier

post.description: Creates a free-tier API key instantly without authentication. The key can be used immediately for all API endpoints. Rate limited to 2 keys per hour per IP. Keys expire after 30 days.

503.description: Feature is currently disabled or temporarily unavailable

post.summary: Transfer NFTs or tokens between wallets
post.description: Returns ordered blockchain actions to execute for transferring one or more NFTs or tokens from one wallet to another. Supports bulk transfers of multiple assets in a single request.

400.description: The request is invalid. Possible reasons: empty assets array, invalid addresses, invalid chain, or cross-ecosystem transfer.

get.summary: Get trait offers for a collection
get.description:
Get trait offers for a collection. Use the `mode` parameter to select the bid category.

**Single string trait:** `?mode=STRING&type=Background&value=Red`
**Multiple string traits:** `?mode=MULTI&traits=[{"traitType":"Background","value":"Red"},{"traitType":"Eyes","value":"Blue"}]`
**Numeric trait range:** `?mode=NUMERIC&type=Level&min_value=1&max_value=10`

Omit filter params to discover all bids of that mode (e.g. `?mode=NUMERIC` returns all numeric bids).

If `mode` is omitted, the mode is inferred from the params for backward compatibility.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Unique string to identify a collection on OpenSea

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Bid category: STRING (single string trait), MULTI (multiple string traits), or NUMERIC (numeric trait range). If omitted, inferred from params.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Trait type name

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Trait value as string

parameters.name: float_value
parameters.description: Trait value as float

parameters.name: int_value
parameters.description: Trait value as integer

parameters.name: min_value
parameters.description: Minimum value for numeric trait range queries

parameters.name: max_value
parameters.description: Maximum value for numeric trait range queries

parameters.name: traits
parameters.description: JSON array of trait filters for multi-trait queries. Each element has 'traitType' and 'value' fields. Example: [{"traitType":"Background","value":"Red"}]

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items to return per page

schema.description: Number of items to return per page

get.description: Get the best listings for a collection sorted by price ascending. Optionally filter by item traits using the 'traits' query parameter with a JSON array of trait filters. Multiple traits are AND-combined (items must match all). Note: results are not deduplicated by token ID — if a token has multiple listings, each listing is returned individually. Filter client-side if you need unique tokens. Example: ?traits=[{"traitType":"Background","value":"Red"}]

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Unique string to identify a collection on OpenSea

parameters.name: include_private_listings
parameters.description: Whether to include private listings; defaults to false

parameters.name: traits
parameters.description: JSON array of trait filters to narrow listings by item traits. Each object has 'traitType' and 'value' fields. Multiple traits are AND-combined (items must match all). Example: [{"traitType":"Background","value":"Red"}]

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of items to return per page

schema.description: Number of items to return per page

get.description: Get time-series floor price data for a collection.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: Number of data points to return

get.description: Get a list of trending NFT collections sorted by sales activity over a specified timeframe. Trending is determined by sales volume and activity metrics. Available timeframes range from 1 minute to all time. For the one_day timeframe without a chain filter, collections are sorted by a composite trending score; all other timeframes sort by raw sales count. Results are filtered to verified, non-spam, non-NSFW collections with minimum volume thresholds.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Time window for trending calculation. Options: one_minute, five_minutes, fifteen_minutes, one_hour, one_day, seven_days, thirty_days, one_year, all_time.

parameters.name: chains
parameters.description: Blockchain(s) to filter by. Comma-separated list of chain identifiers. Unsupported chains are silently ignored; a 400 is returned only if all specified chains are unsupported.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Category to filter by (e.g. art, gaming, memberships, music, pfps, photography, domain-names, virtual-worlds, sports-collectibles, physical-collectibles).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of collections to return (1-100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the 'next' value from a previous response.

get.description: Get top NFT collections ranked by various stats (sorted descending). Unlike /trending, results are not filtered by verification status; spam-tagged collections are excluded via trust-safety enforcement states. Available sort options: one_day_volume, seven_days_volume, thirty_days_volume, floor_price, one_day_sales, seven_days_sales, thirty_days_sales, total_volume, total_sales.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: The stat to sort collections by (always sorted descending). Options: one_day_volume, seven_days_volume, thirty_days_volume, floor_price, one_day_sales, seven_days_sales, thirty_days_sales, total_volume, total_sales

parameters.name: chains
parameters.description: Blockchain(s) to filter by. Comma-separated list of chain identifiers. Unsupported chains are silently ignored; a 400 is returned only if all specified chains are unsupported.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Category to filter by (e.g. art, gaming, memberships, music, pfps, photography, domain-names, virtual-worlds, sports-collectibles, physical-collectibles).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of collections to return (1-100).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination

get.description: Get an OpenSea Account Profile including details such as bio, social media usernames, and profile image.

parameters.name: address_or_username
parameters.description: The blockchain address or username of the account to retrieve

get.description: Resolve an ENS name (e.g. vitalik.eth), OpenSea username, or wallet address to canonical account info including address, username, and ENS name. The ENS path performs both forward resolution (name → address) and reverse lookup (address → canonical primary name). The address and ENS paths perform enrichment calls (username + ENS reverse lookup) in parallel. The username path only needs an ENS reverse lookup after the initial resolution and runs it sequentially. The ENS and username paths require an extra initial call and may be slower than the address path on cache misses.

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: An ENS name (e.g. vitalik.eth), OpenSea username, or wallet address to resolve

get.description: Get fungible token balances for a specific wallet address. Returns quantity (in display units, not raw/wei), USD value, and token metadata for each token held.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Wallet address

parameters.name: chains
parameters.description: Filter by blockchain(s)

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort field (default: usd_value)

parameters.name: sort_direction
parameters.description: Sort direction (default: desc)

parameters.name: disable_spam_filtering
parameters.description: When true, disables OpenSea's heuristic spam filtering and returns tokens that would normally be hidden (low liquidity, dust, flagged-as-spam, etc.). Tokens flagged for trust & safety enforcement or as malicious are still filtered out regardless. Surfaced tokens carry a `status` field on the response indicating why they would have been filtered.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor for next page

ChainIdentifier.description: Blockchain chain identifier. Use the chain slug (e.g., 'ethereum', 'polygon', 'arbitrum', 'optimism', 'base')

AssetQuantityInput.description: An asset with quantity in base units

TransactionIdentifierInput.description: A transaction identifier with optional swap provider

swap_provider.description: The swap provider used (e.g. RELAY, JUPITER, ZERO_EX, LOCAL_BUY_NFT). Required when relay_request_id is not provided. Can be omitted for cross-chain flows that use relay_request_id.

TransactionReceiptRequest.description: Request to get a transaction receipt/status

swap_quote.description: The swap quote that was originally submitted

relay_request_id.description: Relay request ID for cross-chain tracking

AssetIdentifierResponse.description: Asset identifier with chain, contract address, and optional token ID

AssetReceiptResponse.description: Receipt for a single asset in a transaction

amount.description: Amount in the token's native units

TotalSpentResponse.description: Total spent across all assets in a transaction

TransactionReceiptResponse.description: Transaction receipt with status and asset details

status.description: Transaction status: PENDING, SUCCESS, PARTIAL_SUCCESS, or FAILED

failed_asset_receipts.description: Assets that failed to be received

total_spent.description: Total amount spent across all assets

missing_assets.description: Assets that are missing from the receipt

cross_chain_refunded.description: Whether a cross-chain refund was issued

BatchTokensRequest.description: Request body for batch token retrieval by contract identifiers

contracts.description: List of token contract identifiers to retrieve

TokenContractInput.description: A token contract identifier consisting of chain and address

chain.description: The blockchain the token is on

address.description: The contract address of the token

TokenBaseResponse.description: Common token identity fields shared across token responses

address.description: The contract address of the token

chain.description: The blockchain the token is on

name.description: The display name of the token

symbol.description: The ticker symbol of the token

opensea_url.description: URL to the token page on OpenSea

TokenDetailedResponse.description: Detailed token information including stats and social links

address.description: The contract address of the token

chain.description: The blockchain the token is on

name.description: The display name of the token

symbol.description: The ticker symbol of the token

opensea_url.description: URL to the token page on OpenSea

socials.description: Social media links for the token

status.description: Token safety status based on OpenSea's spam-classification rules. `OK` for tokens that pass all safety checks (the normal case). Categories are intentionally broad and may evolve. Possible values, in decreasing severity: `WARNING` (flagged as risky/suspicious — caution advised), `SPAM` (flagged as spam), `LOW_LIQUIDITY` (insufficient liquidity pool reserves), `OK` (passes all checks).

TokenSocialsResponse.description: Social media links for a token

price_change_1h.description: Price change percentage over the last hour

price_change_24h.description: Price change percentage over the last 24 hours

price_change_7d.description: Price change percentage over the last 7 days

price_change_30d.description: Price change percentage over the last 30 days

SwapAssetInput.description: An asset to swap with chain, contract address, and amount

amount.description: Amount in the smallest unit of the token (e.g. wei for ETH). Use "0" for to_assets to receive the market rate.

SwapExecuteRequest.description: Request body for executing a token swap

from_assets.description: Tokens to swap from (at least one required)

to_assets.description: Tokens to swap to (at least one required)

slippage_tolerance.description: Slippage tolerance as a decimal (0.0 to 0.5, default: 0.01)

SwapCostResponse.description: A cost component of the swap

SwapExecuteResponse.description: Response containing quote details and executable swap transactions

quote.description: Price and fee details for the swap

transactions.description: Ordered list of transactions to execute the swap

usd.description: Price impact in USD (negative means user loses value)

percent.description: Price impact as a percentage (negative means user loses value)

SwapQuoteDetails.description: Price and fee details for a swap quote

total_price_usd.description: Total value of tokens received in USD

total_cost_usd.description: Total cost of tokens sent in USD

price_impact.description: Price impact of the swap (null if unavailable)

swap_provider.description: The swap provider that fulfilled this quote (null if unavailable)

recommended_slippage.description: Recommended slippage tolerance based on volatility analysis (null if unavailable)

costs.description: Breakdown of costs for the swap

route_errors.description: Errors encountered for individual swap routes

SwapRouteErrorResponse.description: An error encountered for a specific swap route

SwapTransactionResponse.description: A transaction to be submitted onchain to execute a swap

to.description: The destination address for the transaction

data.description: The transaction data. For EVM chains: hex-encoded calldata. For SVM chains: comma-separated instructions in programId:data format.

value.description: The native token value to send with the transaction

CriteriaRequest.description: Criteria for collection or trait offers. When traits are specified, the offer targets NFTs matching those traits. For supported collections, the identifierOrCriteria (merkle root) will be '0' and trait matching is validated server-side at fulfillment time.

trait.description: Deprecated: Use 'traits' array instead which supports both single and multiple traits.

traits.description: Trait criteria for the offer. Each trait specifies a type and value that target NFTs must match. These traits are validated server-side at fulfillment time.

numericTraits.description: Numeric trait criteria for the offer. Each entry specifies a trait type with optional min/max range bounds. Can be combined with categorical traits.

units_to_fill.description: Optional quantity of units to fulfill; defaults to 1 for offers

include_optional_creator_fees.description: Whether to include optional creator fees in the fulfillment. If creator fees are already required, this is a no-op. Defaults to false.

trait.description: Deprecated: Use 'traits' array instead which supports both single and multiple traits.

criteria.description: The criteria to pass through to the POST /api/v2/offers submit step. Includes collection and trait information so trait offers are not accidentally submitted as collection offers.

encodedTokenIds.description: Encoded token IDs that can be used to fulfill the criteria offer. When identifierOrCriteria is '0', this field is informational only and not required for order construction.

identifierOrCriteria.description: For criteria-based offers, this is the merkle root of eligible token IDs. For trait offers on supported collections, this will be '0' — trait matching is validated server-side at fulfillment time instead of via onchain merkle proof.

BatchNftsRequest.description: Request body for batch NFT retrieval by identifiers

identifiers.description: List of NFT identifiers to retrieve

NftIdentifierInput.description: An NFT identifier consisting of chain, contract address, and token ID

chain.description: The blockchain the NFT is on

contract_address.description: The contract address of the NFT

token_id.description: The token ID of the NFT

CrossChainPaymentToken.description: Payment token to use for cross-chain fulfillment

token_address.description: Contract address of the payment token (use 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 for native token)

SweepCollectionRequest.description: Request to sweep buy items from a collection

collection_slug.description: The slug of the collection to sweep

max_items.description: Maximum number of items to buy (1-50)

max_price_per_item.description: Maximum price per item in the payment token's units

recipient.description: Optional recipient address for the purchased items

SweepCollectionResponse.description: Response containing ordered blockchain actions to execute for a collection sweep

steps.description: Ordered list of blockchain actions to execute. Each action is a JSON object with a single field indicating the type (e.g. buyItemAction, permit2SignatureAction, paymentApprovalAction) and its associated data. Serialized using proto3 JSON format — fields with default values (empty string, 0, false) may be omitted.

errors.description: Errors encountered during sweep. Present alongside steps for partial success cases (e.g. some listings became unavailable).

SweepError.description: An error encountered during a sweep operation

units_to_fill.description: Optional quantity of units to fulfill; defaults to remaining units for listings

include_optional_creator_fees.description: Whether to include optional creator fees in the fulfillment. If creator fees are already required, this is a no-op. Defaults to false.

CrossChainFulfillmentRequest.description: Request to fulfill one or more listings using a payment token on a different chain or a different token on the same chain

listings.description: One or more listings to fulfill

recipient.description: Optional recipient address for the purchased items

CrossChainFulfillmentResponse.description: Response containing ordered transactions to execute for cross-chain fulfillment

transactions.description: Ordered list of transactions to execute. May include approval and buy/swap transactions.

CreateListingActionsRequest.description: Request to get listing creation actions

use_creator_fee.description: Whether to include creator fees. Defaults to true.

taker.description: Optional taker address for private listings

ListingItem.description: An item to list for sale

start_time.description: Listing start time in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to now.

end_time.description: Listing end time in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to 30 days from start.

CreateListingActionsResponse.description: Response containing blockchain actions to execute for listing creation

steps.description: Ordered list of blockchain actions to execute. May include approval actions (e.g. setApprovalForAll) and a createListingsAction containing the Seaport order to sign. Serialized using proto3 JSON format — fields with default values (empty string, 0, false) may be omitted.

minter.description: Wallet address that will receive the minted tokens

drop_type.description: Drop type (see validation error for supported values)

token_type.description: Token type (see validation error for supported values)

BatchCollectionsRequest.description: Request body for batch collection retrieval by slugs

slugs.description: List of collection slugs to retrieve

api_key.description: The API key to use in X-API-KEY header

name.description: Key name for identification and revocation

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the key expires

upgrade_url.description: URL to upgrade to higher rate limits

RateLimitsResponse.description: Rate limits for the API key

chain.description: The chain the asset is on (e.g. ethereum, base, solana)

contract.description: The contract address of the asset

token_id.description: The token ID of the asset

quantity.description: The quantity to transfer in raw units (1 for ERC721, raw amount for ERC1155/fungible)

TransferRequest.description: Request to transfer NFTs or tokens between wallets

TransferResponse.description: Response containing ordered blockchain actions to execute for an asset transfer

steps.description: Ordered list of blockchain actions to execute. Each action is a JSON object with a single field indicating the type (e.g. transferAction, approvalAction) and its associated data. Serialized using proto3 JSON format — fields with default values (empty string, 0, false) may be omitted.

next_stage.description: The earliest upcoming stage by start_time when the drop is not currently minting (e.g. before it starts or between stages). Null if the drop is live or has no future stages.

TokenBalanceResponse.description: Token balance for a wallet address

address.description: The contract address of the token

chain.description: The blockchain the token is on

name.description: The display name of the token

symbol.description: The ticker symbol of the token

opensea_url.description: URL to the token page on OpenSea

quantity.description: Token balance in display units (divided by 10^decimals), not raw/wei

usd_value.description: Total USD value of the balance (quantity * usd_price)

status.description: Token status relative to OpenSea's spam-classification rules. `OK` for tokens that pass all spam filters (the normal case); populated with a more specific value for tokens surfaced via `disable_spam_filtering=true` that would normally be hidden. Categories are intentionally broad and may evolve. Possible values, in decreasing severity: `WARNING` (flagged as risky/suspicious — caution advised), `SPAM` (flagged as spam), `LOW_LIQUIDITY` (insufficient pool liquidity), `LOW_VALUE` (dust holding < $0.01), `OK` (passes all filters).

base_token_liquidity_usd.description: USD value of base token reserves in the top liquidity pool paired with a curated quote token

quote_token_liquidity_usd.description: USD value of quote token reserves in the top liquidity pool paired with a curated quote token

PortfolioStatsResponse.description: Portfolio stats including total value, P&L, and asset breakdown

pnl_absolute.description: Absolute P&L in USD over the timeframe

PortfolioHistoryDataPoint.description: A single net worth data point

BadRequest.description: For error reasons, review the response data.

InternalError.description: Internal server error. Please open a support ticket so OpenSea can investigate.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum requests per rate limit window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix epoch timestamp when the rate limit window resets
